default.$description: The default background color of the page, typically applied to the html or body element.

hover.$description: The background color of hover states that use background-base-color

default.$description: The background color of any surface elements in the UI. Typically applied to Containers, Sections, Cards, Menu's, Modals, Forms or any other container/component that contains UI elements and other components.

hover.$description: Background color for the hover state of background-surface-color

default.$description: The secondary surface color, typically applied to the backgrounds of content cards, sections, and similar components. Secondary surface color should be used when a distinction is needed between its background and that of background-surface-color. For example, alternating row backgrounds in a data table

hover.$description: Background color for the hover state of the background-surface-secondary-color

default.$description: An inverted variant of the default surface color, dark in light mode and light in dark mode. It is used as the background color for components that require a stark contrast with the application background, such as tooltips.

hover.$description: Background color for the hover state of the background-iniverse-color

default.$description: The tertiary surface color, typically used when a distinction is needed between its background and that of both background-surface-color and background-surface-secondary-color.

hover.$description: Background color for the hover state of the background-surface-tertiary-color

default.$description: The background color of elements used to reinforce the brand, typically primary calls to action such as primary buttons, checkboxes, radios, links etc.

hover.$description: Background hover color for primary accent elements.

active.$description: Background active color for primary accent elements.

default.$description: The border color of elements used to reinforce the brand, typically primary calls to action such as primary buttons, checkboxes, radios etc.

hover.$description: Border hover color for primary accent elements.

active.$description: Border active color for primary accent elements.

default.$description: Background color for communicating critical information, such as error messages and alerts.
Border color for communicating critical information, such as error messages and alerts.
Text color for communicating critical information, such as error messages and alerts.
Background color for communicating positive information outcomes, such as success messages and alerts.
Border color for communicating positive information outcomes, such as success messages and alerts.
Text color for communicating positive information outcomes, such as success messages and alerts.
Background color for communicating caution, such as in warning messages and alerts.
Border color for communicating caution, such as in warning messages and alerts.
Text color for communicating caution, such as in warning messages and alerts.
Background color for communicating informative messages that dont have a negative, cautionary or successful semantic meaning.
Border color for communicating informative messages that dont have a negative, cautionary or successful semantic meaning.
Text color for communicating informative messages that dont have a negative, cautionary or successful semantic meaning.
The default border color for neutral elements, such as text inputs, container borders, horizontal separators etc.

hover.$description: Border hover color for border default colors

active.$description: Border active color for border default colors

primary.$description: The primary text color that used throughout the application. This is typically applied to body text and any text that is used on a neutral background color.

inverse.$description: Text color that is used when text is placed on top of the background-inverse-color token.

on-accent.$description: Text color that is used when placed on top of the primary accent color in order to meet accessibility contrast requirements of 4.5:1.

secondary.$description: The secondary text color that used throughout the application. This is typically applied to text that requires less visual emphasis than primary text.

disabled.$description: Text color for any text in a disabled state and thus exempt from user interaction.

default.$description: Background color for UI elements related to destructive actions.

hover.$description: Background hover color for UI elements related to destructive actions.

active.$description: Background active color for UI elements related to destructive actions.

default.$description: Border color for UI elements related to destructive actions.

hover.$description: Border hover color for UI elements related to destructive actions.

active.$description: Border active color for UI elements related to destructive actions.
